decks.notes: This deck focuses on using cheap creatures and removal spells to attack the opponent quickly and efficiently. By using cards like Cast Down and Eviscerator's Insight to deal with threats, while also developing your board with creatures like Refurbished Familiar and Nyxborn Hydra, the deck aims to apply pressure on the opponent early on and maintain control of the board. The land destruction spells like Cleansing Wildfire also disrupt the opponent's strategy, while cards like Nihil Spellbomb provide additional utility and card advantage. The overall strategy is to be aggressive and disruptive, aiming to overwhelm the opponent before they have a chance to stabilize.

The main focus of this deck is to quickly deal damage to the opponent using a combination of direct burn spells and aggressive creatures. The goal is to overwhelm the opponent with a fast and efficient offense, utilizing cards that deal damage directly to the opponent or provide additional value when spells are cast. By maintaining a steady stream of damage through burn spells and creature attacks, the deck aims to put pressure on the opponent and win the game before they have a chance to stabilize.

The main concept of this deck is to control the game by using Gates to ramp up mana and provide access to different colors. The deck aims to control the board with removal spells like Journey to Nowhere and Malevolent Rumble while also using creatures like Sacred Cat and Lunarch Veteran to gain life and maintain board presence. The deck also includes cards like Prismatic Strands and Thraben Charm for protection and utility. Overall, the deck focuses on outlasting the opponent while gradually building up to powerful threats like Crimson Fleet Commodore and Avenging Hunter.

The main goal of this Mono Blue Aggro deck is to establish early board presence with efficient low-cost creatures like Faerie Miscreant and Faerie Seer, backed up by disruptive spells like Counterspell and Snuff Out to control the game. The deck also utilizes Ninjas like Ninja of the Deep Hours to generate card advantage and apply pressure. Thorn of the Black Rose provides card advantage and a late-game threat. The inclusion of Lórien Revealed and Moon-Circuit Hacker offers additional ways to disrupt opponents while pushing for damage. This deck aims to overwhelm opponents with a fast and aggressive game plan.

This deck focuses on low-cost creatures and burn spells to quickly deal damage to the opponent. It also includes a mix of artifacts and utility lands to provide additional card advantage and control. The goal is to maintain a fast pace and apply early pressure to overwhelm the opponent before they have a chance to stabilize. With a good mix of aggressive creatures and removal spells, this deck aims to consistently apply pressure and disrupt the opponent's game plan, leading to a swift victory.

This deck focuses on using countermagic, removal spells, and card draw to control the game. It aims to disrupt the opponent's strategy while efficiently managing its own resources. The inclusion of Gurmag Angler as a finisher adds a threat that can quickly close out the game. The deck is designed to outlast opponents, drawing cards and removing threats until it can establish control and come out on top.

This deck focuses on controlling the opponent's resources and disrupting their plans through card draw, counterspells, and removal spells. The goal is to maintain board control while gradually building card advantage and threatening the opponent with efficient creatures. By utilizing a combination of efficient removal, card draw, and counterspells, the deck aims to outlast and overwhelm the opponent in a battle of attrition, ultimately securing victory through superior card advantage and strategic decision-making.

The main focus of this Pauper Mono Blue Aggro deck is to quickly overwhelm the opponent with efficient creatures and disruptive spells. Utilizing low-cost threats like Delver of Secrets and Tolarian Terror, along with counterspells like Counterspell and Force Spike, the deck aims to maintain control of the game tempo and prevent the opponent from mounting a defense. Card draw and filtering effects like Brainstorm and Ponder help to ensure a steady stream of threats, while endgame finishers like Cryptic Serpent and Lórien Revealed seal the deal for a swift victory.

This deck focuses on overwhelming the opponent with a swarm of small, aggressive creatures in order to quickly deplete their life total. Through efficient use of white creatures and spells, the goal is to maintain a constant presence on the battlefield and keep the pressure on the opponent. The deck also includes cards that provide card advantage and combat tricks to outmaneuver the opponent's defenses. By utilizing a mix of low-cost creatures and spells, this aggressive strategy aims to take control of the game early and maintain the advantage until victory is achieved.

The deck focuses on quickly burning through the opponent's life total using a combination of aggressive creatures and direct damage spells. It aims to build up a board presence with creatures like Kessig Flamebreather and Voldaren Epicure while also casting spells like Lightning Bolt and Fiery Temper to deal direct damage to the opponent. The deck also includes cards like Fireblast and Grab the Prize to finish off the opponent once their life total is low enough. By combining creature aggression with direct damage spells, the deck looks to overwhelm and outpace the opponent in a fast-paced game.

The main focus of this Dimir Control deck is to utilize cheap removal spells and counterspells to disrupt the opponent's game plan while also generating card advantage through cards like Augur of Bolas and brainstorm. The deck also aims to establish a strong board presence with creatures like Faerie Seer and Spellstutter Sprite, all while maintaining control of the game with cards like Snuff Out and Counterspell. The inclusion of cards like Thorn of the Black Rose and Ninja of the Deep Hours helps generate additional value and card advantage. Additionally, the deck utilizes lands like Bojuka Bog to disrupt graveyards and gain an advantage in attrition-based matchups.

The main focus of this deck is to generate mana quickly and efficiently in order to cast powerful spells that can overwhelm the opponent in the early game. By utilizing cards like Manamorphose, Thornscape Familiar, and Glimpse the Impossible, the deck aims to create explosive turns that can lead to a rapid victory. The inclusion of cards like Seething Song and Rite of Flame further enhance the deck's ability to generate large amounts of mana in a short amount of time. Overall, the goal is to assemble the necessary resources to execute the powerful Ruby Storm combo and secure a win.

The UR Control deck focuses on maintaining board control through counter spells, removal, and card draw. By using efficient countermagic like Counterspell and Spell Pierce, along with removal spells like Skred and Lightning Bolt, the deck aims to disrupt the opponent's game plan while generating card advantage with cards like Ponder and Brainstorm. Murmuring Mystic provides a win condition by creating bird tokens, while Lórien Revealed and Crimson Fleet Commodore offer additional value. The deck also includes land ramp and fixing with cards like Seething Landscape and Volatile Fjord to ensure consistent mana production.

The main focus of this Dimir Control deck is to disrupt and control the opponent's plays while generating card advantage through spells like Deep Analysis and Brainstorm. The deck utilizes a mix of removal spells, counterspells, and card draw to maintain control of the game. The presence of creatures like Gurmag Angler and Murmuring Mystic provide additional threats on the board, while cards like Lórien Revealed and Tolarian Terror offer additional utility. Overall, the deck aims to outmaneuver and outlast opponents through careful resource management and strategic decision-making.

The main focus of this deck is to quickly deploy a board of Elf creatures and utilize various synergies involving mana production, creature pumping, and card draw to overwhelm the opponent with an aggressive strategy. By playing efficient and cheap Elf creatures along with cards like Timbewatch Elf and Quirion Ranger, the deck aims to generate a lot of mana and push damage through. Lead the Stampede and Winding Way help refill the hand with Elves, while Masked Vandal provides removal and Nyxborn Hydra acts as a powerful finisher.

This deck focuses on quickly ramping up mana with cards like Utopia Sprawl and Arbor Elf, allowing for early game aggression with powerful creatures like Nyxborn Hydra and Avenging Hunter. Cards like Ram Through and Malevolent Rumble help to clear the way for your creatures to attack, while Writhing Chrysalis provides a way to generate more threats. The deck aims to overwhelm opponents with a combination of big creatures and removal spells, leading to a fast and aggressive playstyle that can quickly close out games.
